task_completion.description: Create tsconfig.json with strict mode and esModuleInterop enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer shows tsconfig.json in the project root. Editor shows 'strict': true and 'esModuleInterop': true within the file.

task_completion.description: Update package.json with TypeScript devDependencies and build script.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Editor showing package.json with 'typescript' in 'devDependencies' and a 'build': 'tsc' entry in 'scripts'.

task_completion.description: Rename all .js files in src/ to .ts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer tree view shows the files inside the 'src/' directory have .ts extensions instead of .js.

task_completion.description: Define explicit TypeScript interfaces for Book and User.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Editor shows TypeScript code defining 'interface Book { ... }' and 'interface User { ... }'.

task_completion.description: Add proper type annotations to Express middleware and route handlers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Editor shows route handlers and middleware with explicit types, such as '(req: Request, res: Response)' or '(req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction)'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the project compiles with zero errors using npx tsc --noEmit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing the command 'npx tsc --noEmit' executed and returning to the prompt with no error output.

integrity.description: Agent did not abuse the 'any' type to bypass type checking.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible TypeScript code does not show widespread use of ': any' (task restricts it to a maximum of two uses).

integrity.description: Agent did not remove core business logic to achieve zero compilation errors.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible route handlers still contain functional logic (e.g., data manipulation, responses) rather than being emptied out or stubbed.

integrity.description: Agent used a real text editor or IDE to perform the refactoring.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an IDE/editor (like VS Code) being used to navigate and edit the codebase, rather than just terminal echo/sed commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires a passing score of 60 points. The workspace is located at /home/ga/workspace/bookshelf-api/. The agent must act as a Web Developer in the SaaS industry.
